segments.name: Institutional Investors & Allocators
segments.description: Fund managers, family offices, and institutional allocators evaluating AI exposure
pain_points: Can't distinguish durable AI moats from temporary model leads
AI fund landscape is undifferentiated — 'buying Mag 7 with extra steps'
Need defensible AI thesis for LP meetings
Information overload from AI newsletters with declining signal-to-noise
Missed NVDA/crypto runs, terrified of missing the next structural shift

segments.name: AI Infrastructure Operators
segments.description: Founders and executives building compute, data, and energy infrastructure for AI
pain_points: Can't find operators who understand both infrastructure AND markets
Difficulty communicating infrastructure constraints to investors

fears: Running out of compute/power before hitting scale
desires: Validation from someone who's done it

segments.name: Senior Technical Leaders at AI Labs
segments.description: Research leads and engineering directors at frontier AI companies
pain_points: Models converging — architecture no longer the differentiator
Data scarcity for specialized domains (robotics, embodied AI)
Gap between research results and commercial deployment

segments.name: AI Supply Chain Founders
segments.description: Founders building companies in data, compute, energy, robotics for AI
pain_points: Isolated in one layer of the stack — can't see the full picture
Don't know where their layer fits in the broader AI value chain
Need frameworks to communicate value to investors

off_limits: Specific revenue numbers ($ARR, exact revenue) — use proof points instead
Point72 as a named customer — reference 'top quant funds' generically
Positioning Gerra as deploying physical robots to customers (it's a software/data company)
Negative mentions of specific competitors by name
